name: Context / in-situ photography
logic: Buyers buy an outcome, not an object. A product on a white background answers 'what is it'; the same product on a café counter, a job site, or a human answers 'is this right for MY use case' — the question that actually closes. Application and in-situ shots let a buyer self-qualify at a glance. Baymard's research shows products presented only in isolation leave users unable to judge real-world fit and suitability, especially for anything used in a setting.

evidence.title: Provide Images of Products on a Human Model / in Context
evidence.note: Isolation-only imagery leaves users unable to judge fit and real-world suitability; context (model, environment) restores it.

evidence.title: 'In Scale' Images Use Relevant Surroundings
evidence.note: Recommends showing the product 'relative to a surrounding environment, humans, or other objects' (verified 2026-06-11).

exemplars.name: IKEA room sets
exemplars.why: The canon of in-situ merchandising — products shown in fully styled, lived-in rooms.
